revision.comment: per WP:MOSSIS "Do not make a section whose sole content is box-type templates"
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: per WP:MOSSIS "Do not make a section whose sole content is box-type templates"
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude>

Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude>

Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! -->

Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! -->

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was 1 revision imported: overwriting
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! -->
[[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]] [[Category:templates missing wikidata]]</noinclude>

revision.comment: per WP:MOSSIS "Do not make a section whose sole content is box-type templates"
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: 1 revision imported: importing [[Template:Commons category]] from English wikipedia with removed timestamps to overwrite existing code, attempting to fix an error that makes the link not properly appear.
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude>

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}} <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "{{Documentation}}" to "{{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]]"
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude> [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 1 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23176857634|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! --> </noinclude> <noinclude> [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

[[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! -->
[[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>

revision.comment: [[Project:History cleanup|Offloading history due to miraheze stability concerns]] many contributors, see [https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Commons_templates_location here] for full history [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!-- History offloaded: due to miraheze stability concerns, the edit history of this page has been offloaded to the XML archive. See https://emmaleonhart.github.io/shintowiki-scripts/wikihistory.html?page=Commons_templates_location for full history. Last refreshed: [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]] --> [[File:Sister link template columns problem.jpg|thumb|If using a large, graphical template produces odd layout problems, like the excessive white space shown on the right of this screenshot, then switch to a different ''sister template''.]] Generally: :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template or any box-type template in a section all by itself.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not place this template in a section containing columns without floating left.''' :{{xmark}} '''Do not indent this template with colon (:) or a bullet ({{asterisk}}) markup.'''
Specifically: * In articles, this template should be placed at the ''top'' of the last section on the page so that it floats to the right of that section. ** See [[Wikipedia:Wikimedia sister projects#Where to place links]] for more information and alternatives. * In [[WP:disambiguation pages|disambiguation pages]], this template is usually placed at the top of the page. * In categories, this template should be grouped with other templates at the beginning of the category introduction. This avoids unnecessary white space as any text is floated around the template boxes.<noinclude> {{Documentation}}[[Category:Templates with Documentation template]] <!-- Add categories and interwikis to the /doc subpage, not here! -->
[[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]] </noinclude>
